topic.what: Integral cryptanalysis of Kalyna block cipher variants with reduced rounds
topic.how: Tracking propagation of carefully chosen plaintext multisets through SPN round structure combined with backward extension and partial decryption for key recovery
topic.why_matters: First integral analysis covering all Kalyna variants; improves data complexity from 2^96 to 2^8 for distinguishers; provides unified understanding of modular whitening impact on integral properties

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of chosen plaintexts needed for attacks
characteristics.vs_baseline: Improves from prior 2^96 to 2^8 for Kalyna-128 distinguishers
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 shows 4-round distinguisher reduced from 2^96 to 2^8
characteristics.context: Different attacks require different data complexities ranging from 2^8 to 2^17

characteristics.vs_baseline: Key-recovery attacks on 5-round Kalyna-128/128 in 2^74 time
characteristics.context: Varies by attack type: weak-key 4-round at 2^74 vs standard 5-round at 2^74 for Kalyna-128/128

characteristics.vs_baseline: Prior MITM attacks covered 7-11 rounds but with different complexity profile
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 summarizes rounds attacked from 2 to 6 across variants
characteristics.context: Integral attacks extend from 2-round distinguishers to 4-6 round key-recovery

characteristics.evidence: All reported attacks specify negligible memory
characteristics.context: Unlike MITM attacks requiring up to 2^203 bits memory

characteristics.what_it_measures: Effect of omitting pre-whitening on attack capability
characteristics.evidence: 5-round attack without pre-whitening vs 4-round with pre-whitening for Kalyna-128
characteristics.context: Pre-whitening with modular addition in Kalyna affects integral property propagation

characteristics.evidence: 2^8 multiset based on single active byte at MSB position
characteristics.context: Small structured multisets sufficient due to modular arithmetic properties

novelty: first integral analysis of Kalyna-256/256 and Kalyna-512/512
unified treatment across all three block sizes
novel propagation analysis with modular addition
weak-key integral distinguishers for extended rounds
characterization of 64-bit column as natural recovery unit
open_problems: extension to full-round attacks remains open
pre-whitening generalization beyond Kalyna not explored
automation via MILP not fully developed for Kalyna specific features
